title: Scones – only 3 ingredients
excerpt: I like scones, but they shouldn't taste of baking powder. That leaves such a rough taste in your mouth. To my mind scones should rise high and not look doughy on the inside. I came across this recipe on my friend Pierre Rossouw's Facebook page. I was doubtful about the strange three ingredients …
html:
I like scones, but they shouldn't taste of baking powder. That leaves such a rough taste in your mouth. To my mind  scones  should rise high and not look doughy on the inside.  I came across this recipe on my friend Pierre Rossouw's Facebook page.  I was doubtful about the strange three ingredients.  Well, I put it to the test and I'll never look back. Delicious, delicious, delicious.

I know in Afrikaans it's called "botterbroodjies", but that doesn't sit as nicely on my tongue.

Look, now that was lovely.  We enjoyed it with strawberry jam and cream.  You can also enjoy it savoury, with cheese. Here you can get wonderfully creative.  Make a tuna mixture with parsley and mayo to spoon on top, chicken mayonnaise, chopped boiled egg – whatever your heart desires.

I'm sure you can also stir in chocolate chips, nuts, chopped dried fruit, olives  , sun-dried tomatoes and anything else of your choice.

It's so quick and easy that you'll never have to lie awake again wondering what to feed your guests for breakfast.

Even Sus, our dog, gets spoiled with one in her own little bowl – that misshapen one you set aside.

items: 330 ml Sprite cooldrink (a can)
And then a bit of salt (half a teaspoon).

steps: Pour your flour and salt into a mixing bowl and make a well in the middle with your wooden spoon.
Pour the cream into the well and stir together.
Now add your Sprite and stir well.
Spray your muffin pan with spray and cook, or grease it with butter, then spoon it into a muffin pan (you'll easily get 12 out of it).
Bake for 20 – 25 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius.
